title: Reuse checkout auth for release distribution checks

sources.reason: Release distribution validation needs to reuse checkout-auth headers so private GitHub CI can clone the tagged release.
Regression coverage must confirm both checkout-auth reuse and explicit public repository overrides.

riskAssessment.reason: The fix changes how release validation reuses GitHub auth context without changing public release semantics.

executionDecision.reason: The release auth reuse fix is concrete and verifiable.

preReviewWarnings: Verify that no credential material is echoed into logs.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: make check-release-distribution can run in a private repository CI environment.
The release distribution check still works in a public repository environment.
Regression tests cover checkout-auth headers and explicit repository overrides.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The release distribution check fails in private GitHub CI because the clone step does not reuse the authenticated checkout context provided by actions/checkout.
constraints: Do not change the public release validation semantics beyond how Git auth is reused.
Keep the check reproducible in CI and locally.
Do not expose credential material in logs.
intent.rationale: Reusing the checkout-auth context keeps the public release verification path intact while making it work in private-repo CI.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
